Young people generally want to be independent and have early possession of wealth and power. But this often ruins their future comfort and causes harm to others. It is much happier when young people are cared for by parents or guardians until age gives them experience and good judgment. Although these young people may not be indulged as much, they will later be thankful. Manasseh did many evil things in the sight of the Lord, seemingly intending to provoke him to anger; he did more wrong than the nations whom the Lord destroyed. Manasseh continued from bad to worse until he was taken captive to Babylon. The people were ready to agree with his wishes, to gain his favor, and because it suited their corrupt desires. In reforming large groups of people, numbers are merely those who serve at the convenience of others, and in temptation they fall away.

Here is the judgment on Judah and Jerusalem. The words used represent the city emptied and utterly desolate, yet not destroyed by this, but cleansed, and to be kept for the future dwelling of the Jews: forsaken, yet not finally, and only as to outward privileges, because individual believers were preserved in that visitation. The Lord will reject any people who claim to believe in him but dishonor him with their crimes, but he will never abandon his cause on earth. In the book of Chronicles we read of Manasseh’s repentance and acceptance with God; thus we may learn not to despair of the recovery of even the greatest sinners. But let none dare to continue in sin, presuming that they may repent and reform when they choose. There are a few examples of the conversion of notorious sinners so that no one should despair; and few, so that no one should presume.

Amon desecrated God’s house with his idols; and God allowed his house to be defiled with his blood. However unjust those were who did it, God was just in allowing it to happen. Now came a happy change from one of the worst to one of the best kings of Judah. Once more Judah was tested with a reformation. Whether the Lord bears long with presumptuous offenders or quickly cuts them off in their sins, all must perish who persist in refusing to walk in his ways.
